summary: An OS manages computer hardware and software resources
Acts as an interface between user and hardware
Handles process scheduling, memory management, and I/O
Common types: Windows, macOS, Linux, Android
Key concepts: multitasking, virtual memory, file systems
ML is a subset of AI that learns from data
Three types: Supervised, Unsupervised, Reinforcement Learning
Common algorithms: Linear Regression, Decision Trees, Neural Networks
Requires training data, features, and labels
Applications: image recognition, NLP, recommendation systems
Organized ways to store and access data efficiently
Linear: Arrays, Linked Lists, Stacks, Queues
Choice depends on use case and time complexity
Fundamental for algorithm design and optimization
